paper_title: Birch pollen influence the severity of atopic eczema – prospective clinical cohort pilot study and ex vivo penetration study

methods.description: Process for recruiting and categorizing patients based on their sensitization to birch pollen for a clinical study on atopic eczema.

steps.description: The study was performed in 2009 at two dermatological centers in Northern Germany (in Kiel and Hannover). For this pilot study, we aimed to enroll as many adult atopic patients from the dermatologic outpatient clinic that fulfilled the Criteria of Hanifin and Rajka.
steps.searchable_summary: Recruit adult atopic patients from dermatologic outpatient clinics in Kiel and Hannover.

steps.description: Subjects were consecutively recruited from March 2009 until 3 days before the start of the birch pollen season, as determined by the institute of the national ministry of traffic and digital infrastructure.
steps.searchable_summary: Recruit patients until 3 days before birch pollen season starts.

steps.description: The eligible subjects were divided by their status of specific immunoglobulin (Ig)E against birch pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Divide patients based on IgE status against birch pollen.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring daily amounts of birch pollen in the air using a Burkhard pollen trap.

steps.description: The daily amounts of birch pollen in the air were determined with the aid of the Burkhard pollen trap.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure daily birch pollen levels using Burkhard trap.

steps.description: Analysis was performed with light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen samples using light microscopy.

methods.description: Procedure for labeling grass pollen proteins with a fluorescent dye for experimental investigations.

steps.description: Two ampoules with 1.26 mg extract of grass pollen were dissolved in 1 mL of phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve grass pollen extract in PBS buffer.

steps.description: A 10 mM solution of fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC) was added to the pollen extract.
steps.searchable_summary: Add FITC solution to pollen extract.

steps.description: This mixture was incubated in the dark for 2 hours at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate FITC-labeled pollen solution in the dark.

steps.description: The FITC-labeled pollen proteins were purified using membrane dialysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Purify FITC-labeled proteins using membrane dialysis.

methods.description: Experimental procedure to investigate the penetration of pollen allergens into skin explants.

steps.description: Grass pollen proteins, as used for the prick test, were applied to the skin.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply grass pollen proteins to skin explants.

steps.description: Laser scanning microscopy was used to identify CD1c+ epidermal cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify CD1c+ cells using laser scanning microscopy.

steps.description: Fluorescence was observed in cells after excitation at 488 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe fluorescence in cells at 488 nm excitation.
